REJECT if: generic fonts, purple gradient on white, looks like typical AI-generated UI, cookie-cutter layout, no memorable element, timid color palette, predictable patterns. If rejected, provide a revised direction. If approved, push it further. Output a hardened aesthetic plan. local.spec_content:
---
id: 1778345000-dracula-vitepress-modernise
title: VitePress Dracula Theme
status: accepted
author: Starscream
created: 2026-05-09
reviewed_by: Starscream
---

## Summary

Modernise the VitePress documentation site at `docs/`. Move the default Inter-on-white aesthetic to a Dracula-inspired dark identity with restrained corners. The site should feel native to the developer audience the CLI targets ‚Äî terminal-adjacent, decisive, distinctive ‚Äî without becoming a parody. Default colour scheme is dark; light mode remains supported but is the secondary experience.

## Requirements

- Dark theme uses a Dracula-derived palette: deep purple/indigo background, soft cream foreground, magenta/cyan/green/yellow accents in the canonical Dracula slots.
- Light theme stays usable but feels like a daytime variant of the same family ‚Äî same accent hues, lighter substrate.
- Border radius is tightened across the board: cards, buttons, custom blocks, code fences. The previous "soft pill" feel is gone; default is `2px` for tight elements, `4px` for cards and code blocks. No element exceeds `4px` except the search modal (which keeps VitePress defaults).
- Hero gradient on the homepage is replaced. The current purple-to-cyan gradient stays in spirit but uses Dracula hues (Pink `#ff79c6` ‚Üí Purple `#bd93f9` ‚Üí Cyan `#8be9fd`) and the angle changes to vertical-ish (`160deg`) so the cyan reads as a "dawn" sliver at the bottom.
- Typography:
  - **Body:** `IBM Plex Sans` (loaded from CDN). Distinctive without being decorative; reads well at long-form length.
  - **Mono:** `IBM Plex Mono` (already widely used in dev tooling, pairs with Sans).
  - **Headings:** Body inherits `IBM Plex Sans` at heavier weights ‚Äî no separate display font.
- Default code-block syntax theme matches the body theme: dark uses the Dracula palette directly; light uses the official Dracula light-style adaptation (`Dracula Pro Day` colours).
- Custom block accents (tip / warning / danger) re-use Dracula palette: tip ‚Üí cyan, warning ‚Üí yellow, danger ‚Üí red.
- The home-page feature cards drop their default subtle borders for a flat, slightly inset look (1px outline matching `--vp-c-divider`, no shadow on hover, lift on hover via translate only).
- Mermaid diagrams (via `vitepress-mermaid-renderer`) honour the dark/light split. Theme passed to the renderer becomes `dark` (Dracula-tuned) on dark mode and `neutral` on light ‚Äî `forest` (default) is wrong for this aesthetic.

## Technical Approach

All work in two files:

1. **`docs/.vitepress/theme/style.css`** ‚Äî set CSS variables for both `:root` (light) and `.dark` selectors. Override the VitePress palette, button colours, custom-block colours, hero gradient, code-block backgrounds, border radii.
2. **`docs/.vitepress/theme/index.ts`** ‚Äî adjust the mermaid renderer theme arg from `forest` to `neutral` on light, keep `dark` on dark.

Font loading: `<link>` to Google Fonts CDN for `IBM Plex Sans` and `IBM Plex Mono`. Inject via vitepress `transformHead` config in `docs/.vitepress/config.ts`, not via CSS `@import` (HEAD link is faster and avoids render-blocking).

Border radius is centralised on a few CSS variables defined at `:root`:

```css
--abtree-radius-tight: 2px;
--abtree-radius-card: 4px;
--abtree-radius-pill: 999px;  /* badges only */
```

VitePress's own `--vp-c-*` and `--vp-c-bg` variables are the override targets ‚Äî no global selector hacks. The default theme's structural rules (layout, spacing, breakpoints) stay untouched.

## Affected Systems

- `docs/.vitepress/theme/style.css` ‚Äî full rewrite
- `docs/.vitepress/theme/index.ts` ‚Äî mermaid theme arg change
- `docs/.vitepress/config.ts` ‚Äî add `transformHead` to inject font links

No source code, no CLI, no tree definitions. Static-site styling only.

## Acceptance Criteria

- `bun run docs:build` completes with no errors.
- Loading `docs/.vitepress/dist/index.html` in dark mode shows: deep-purple background (`#282a36` Dracula bg or close), cream foreground, pink/cyan accents on links and buttons, hero gradient using the three named Dracula hues.
- Loading the same page in light mode shows: warm off-white background, same accent hues at lower saturation, no jarring shift in identity.
- All four feature cards on the home page have `4px` corners and a 1px outline that matches the divider colour.
- Hero CTA button has `2px` corners, not the default `20px` pill.
- Code fences use the Dracula palette in dark mode (visible by inspecting any `<pre>` block).
- IBM Plex Sans/Mono are the active fonts (verifiable in DevTools Computed tab).
- Mermaid diagram on `/getting-started` renders with Dracula-toned colours in dark mode.
- The hello-world execution diagram from getting-started visually reads well in both modes ‚Äî green nodes still pop, uncoloured nodes don't disappear into the background.

## Risks & Considerations

- **Contrast on coloured nodes in mermaid.** Mermaid's own theme override may clash with the per-node `style fill:#4ade80` we set. Mitigation: leave the inline styles as-is (they pin the success-green); only the *un-styled* nodes adopt the palette colours.
- **Font CDN dependency.** First paint depends on Google Fonts. Acceptable trade-off for a docs site; preconnect via `transformHead` mitigates flash.
- **Light mode being an afterthought.** The Dracula brand is dark-first. Light mode will feel like a derivation. Acceptable per the "secondary experience" framing in requirements.
- **Search modal border radius.** VitePress uses an internal Algolia-flavoured modal; overriding its radius is brittle. Documented exception above.

## Open Questions

- None. Bounded to two files, no architectural decisions, single-author repo.
local.context_analysis:
# Context Analysis

## Purpose
A documentation site for abtree, an agentic CLI for behaviour trees. Audience is developers and AI tooling teams ‚Äî terminal-native, opinionated about typography, expect dark mode by default. The site competes for attention against generic VitePress builds; identity matters as much as content.

## Technical Constraints
- VitePress 2.x (alpha), running on Bun. Custom theme extends DefaultTheme.
- Existing plugins: `vitepress-mermaid-renderer` (interactive diagrams, theme-aware), `vitepress-plugin-llms` (llms.txt build artefact).
- Mermaid renderer takes a `theme` arg per render ‚Äî must update mermaid theme along with the visual identity.
- Pages already in production: `/`, `/getting-started`, `/concepts/*`, `/guide/*`, `/examples`. Theme change must work for all without per-page rules.
- Accessibility: AA contrast on body text in both modes; focus rings preserved (no `outline: none` cleanup).
- Performance: docs site, no SSR runtime concerns. Font loading via CDN preconnect is acceptable trade-off.

## Scale
Full site theme refresh ‚Äî spans every page through CSS variable overrides. Not per-component scoped. Two files of styling code (`style.css`, `theme/index.ts`) plus a config tweak for font preconnect.

## Concrete Targets
- Dark mode primary identity: Dracula palette (`#282a36` bg / `#f8f8f2` fg / `#bd93f9` purple / `#ff79c6` pink / `#8be9fd` cyan / `#50fa7b` green / `#f1fa8c` yellow / `#ff5555` red / `#ffb86c` orange).
- Light mode variant: `Dracula Pro Day` adaptation ‚Äî warm off-white substrate (`#f8f8f2`-ish), the same accent hues at lower saturation.
- Border-radius scale: 2px tight / 4px card / 999px badge. Default theme uses 6-12px which reads soft; we want crisper.
- Typography: IBM Plex Sans + IBM Plex Mono via Google Fonts. Replace VitePress's default Inter stack.
- Hero gradient: Dracula pink ‚Üí purple ‚Üí cyan, vertical-ish (`160deg`).

## Out of scope (per spec)
- Search modal radius (Algolia internals).
- Light-first design considerations (light is secondary).
- Per-page CSS overrides.
local.aesthetic_direction:
# Aesthetic Direction

## One-sentence direction
**A dracula-darkened terminal you can read in.** A documentation site that looks like a fixed-pitch developer's home dotfile rendered through a typographer's eye ‚Äî not a marketing site, not a Linear clone, not a default VitePress.

## Typography
- **Body:** `IBM Plex Sans` (400, 500, 600, 700). Distinctive geometry ‚Äî the `a`, `g`, and `R` are unmistakable. Reads at long-form length.
- **Mono:** `IBM Plex Mono` (400, 500, 600). Sister font to the Sans ‚Äî they share humanist proportions, so code blocks don't feel like a different document.
- **Headings:** Plex Sans at 600/700, slight negative letter-spacing on h1/h2 only (`-0.015em`). No display font.
- **Code in prose:** Plex Mono, slight tinted background (Dracula `--current-line` colour at 60% in dark, equivalent in light).

Banned: Inter, Roboto, Arial, system-ui as defaults.

## Color palette (committed Dracula)

### Dark (primary)
```css
--bg              : #282a36   /* base */
--bg-alt          : #21222c   /* code blocks, sidebars */
--bg-soft         : #44475a   /* cards, code inline */
--current-line    : #44475a   /* hover/selection */
--fg              : #f8f8f2   /* body */
--fg-muted        : #6272a4   /* secondary text, line numbers */
--accent-pink     : #ff79c6   /* primary brand, links, hero */
--accent-purple   : #bd93f9   /* secondary brand, h-rule, focus */
--accent-cyan     : #8be9fd   /* tip, info, code attribute names */
--accent-green    : #50fa7b   /* success, code function names */
--accent-yellow   : #f1fa8c   /* warning, code strings */
--accent-orange   : #ffb86c   /* code numbers, special accents */
--accent-red      : #ff5555   /* danger, error */
```

Brand chain: pink (links/CTA) ‚Üí purple (hover/active) ‚Üí cyan (tip/info). Hero gradient: pink ‚Üí purple ‚Üí cyan, `160deg`.

### Light (secondary, derived)
Warm off-white substrate `#f8f8f2`, foreground `#282a36`, accents at ~85% saturation of the dark hues. Pink stays as brand. The site identity is preserved across modes because the *accents* don't shift, only the substrate inverts.

## Motion strategy
- Page transitions: none (VitePress default, browser-native).
- Hover lifts: 80ms `translateY(-2px)` only. No scale, no shadow bloom, no bounce. Crisp.
- Mermaid diagram: zoom/pan handled by the renderer plugin. Cap at 200ms easing on built-in transitions.
- Link underlines: animated `text-decoration-skip-ink` on hover via CSS pseudo, 100ms.
- Focus rings: instant (no transition). 2px solid pink, offset 2px. Always visible, no `outline: none` cleanup.

## Spatial composition
- VitePress's grid stays. We're not redesigning layout, we're recolouring + retypographing.
- Border-radius rationing: **2px** for buttons, badges-as-blocks, code-fence corners; **4px** for feature cards and call-out blocks; **999px** ONLY for status pills and avatars. Default theme's 6-12px is gone.
- Feature cards (homepage): 1px outline `var(--abtree-divider)`, no shadow at rest, hover = `translateY(-2px)` + outline brightens to `var(--accent-purple)`.
- Code blocks: no left border accent (default theme's pink stripe is removed for cleanness); the dark substrate alone signals it's code.

## Background / texture
- Body: solid `#282a36`. No noise, no gradient, no scanlines.
- Hero block only: a faint vertical scanline overlay at 4% opacity (`linear-gradient(to bottom, transparent 50%, rgba(255,255,255,0.04) 50%)` at 3px stripes). Keeps the rest of the site clean while giving the hero one unique surface.
- Hero gradient image (the blurred orb behind the name): swap the default `#bd34fe ‚Üí #41d1ff` for `#ff79c6 ‚Üí #bd93f9 ‚Üí #8be9fd`. Same blur (`44‚Äì68px`). Same angle (`-45deg`).

## ONE memorable thing
**Animated cursor block on the hero name.** After the typed-in title finishes (the page-load gradient text), a single `IBM Plex Mono ‚ñà` block follows the last letter and blinks at a 1.06s interval ‚Äî same cadence as a default xterm cursor. This is the single moment of typography crossover (Sans ‚Üí Mono) that signals "this is for terminal people". No other animation on the home page.

## Why this won't look generic
- Typography commits to Plex (no Inter/Roboto safety net).
- Background is solid Dracula `#282a36` ‚Äî not gradient washed, not soft beige, not Linear-grey.
- Border radius drops to 2-4px ‚Äî most modern docs sit at 8-12px.
- Hero scanline + blink cursor ‚Äî neither is in any VitePress site I've seen.
- Brand colour is **pink**, not the safe purple-or-blue every dev tool defaults to.
local.hardened_design:
# Hardened Design (Director's pass)

The direction holds. Pink-as-brand instead of purple-or-blue is brave ‚Äî most dev-tooling sites default to indigo because it's safe. Plex commits to typography. The blink cursor is a real memorable element. **No reject.** Pushing further.

---

## Approved (unchanged)

- IBM Plex Sans + Plex Mono. Loaded via Google Fonts CDN with preconnect.
- Dracula palette as named, dark-first.
- Border radius rationing: 2px / 4px / 999px.
- Pink as brand colour for links, primary CTA, focus rings.
- Hero scanline overlay at 4% opacity, 3px stripe.
- Animated `‚ñà` mono cursor following the hero name.

## Pushed further

### Selection colour
Most sites leave `::selection` at the browser default (a generic blue). Override it.
- Dark: `background: #ff79c6; color: #282a36;` ‚Äî pink with substrate-coloured text.
- Light: `background: #ffb86c; color: #282a36;` ‚Äî orange. Different colour per mode is intentional; it's a cheap surprise.

### Scrollbar
Claim it. Custom-styled across both `webkit` and `firefox`:
- Track: `var(--bg-alt)`.
- Thumb: `var(--accent-cyan)` at 60% opacity, hover at 100%.
- Width: 10px. Not slim, not chunky. Visible.

### Code-fence language label
Top-right of every fenced code block:
- `font-family: var(--vp-font-family-mono);` (Plex Mono).
- `font-size: 0.7rem`, `text-transform: uppercase`, `letter-spacing: 0.08em`.
- `color: var(--accent-pink);`.
- Background: transparent. No box. Just the word floating.
This is a Plex Mono moment that proves the typographic crossover is intentional, not accidental.

### Header hairline
The site header gets a 1px bottom border of `var(--accent-purple)` at 25% opacity. Default VitePress uses a grey divider; the purple tint is the only place purple "speaks" outside of hover states, and it ties the header to the brand without shouting.

### Table striping
Default theme uses row dividers. Replace with alternating row backgrounds:
- Even rows: `var(--bg)`.
- Odd rows: `var(--bg-alt)`.
- No row-divider lines. Cleaner ‚Äî reads like `ls` output, not a finance dashboard.

### Mermaid theme bridging
The diagram on `/getting-started` uses pre-set `style fill:` declarations for green success nodes. Those win against any theme override (mermaid honours inline styles). For the un-styled nodes (none in hello-world, but possible in future trees), the renderer theme is:
- Dark: `theme: 'dark'` (mermaid's official dark ‚Äî closer to Dracula than `forest`).
- Light: `theme: 'neutral'` (greyscale; doesn't fight the substrate).
Confirmed in spec.

### Inline-code background
Default theme renders `inline code` with a pale grey background. Replace:
- Dark: `background: rgba(255, 121, 198, 0.12);` (pink at 12%).
- Light: `background: rgba(189, 147, 249, 0.10);` (purple at 10%).
The colour shift between modes echoes the selection-colour shift ‚Äî same playbook, different element.

### Custom block headers
The "tip", "warning", "danger" block titles default to brand colour. Override per type:
- Tip: cyan title, cyan icon.
- Warning: yellow title, yellow icon.
- Danger: red title, red icon.
Each block keeps the 4px corner.

## Implementation order

1. Add the font preconnect + load via `transformHead` in `docs/.vitepress/config.ts`.
2. Rewrite `docs/.vitepress/theme/style.css` with the full variable map and component overrides.
3. Update `docs/.vitepress/theme/index.ts` to switch the mermaid theme arg from `forest` to `neutral` for light.
4. Add a tiny Vue render of the `‚ñà` blink-cursor sibling to the hero ‚Äî only on the home page. If implementing this requires more than ~10 lines of theme code, defer it to a follow-up; the rest of the design holds without it.
5. `bun run docs:build` until clean.

## Quality gates (must pass)

- All four feature cards: 1px purple-on-hover outline, 4px corner, translateY(-2px) on hover.
- Hero CTA button: 2px corner, pink fill, dark substrate text.
- Code fence: language label top-right in Plex Mono pink small-caps.
- `::selection` is pink in dark mode.
- Scrollbar thumb is cyan.
- Body font-family computed = IBM Plex Sans.
- Mermaid diagram readable in both modes ‚Äî green success nodes still pop against `#282a36`.
local.implementation:
# Implementation Summary

Three files touched. No new dependencies (Plex via Google Fonts CDN is not a dep).

## docs/.vitepress/theme/style.css ‚Äî full rewrite
- `:root` and `.dark` blocks define the Dracula palette as CSS variables (`--abtree-pink`, `--abtree-purple`, `--abtree-cyan`, ‚Ä¶) plus radius scale (`--abtree-radius-tight: 2px`, `--abtree-radius-card: 4px`, `--abtree-radius-pill: 999px`).
- VitePress's own `--vp-c-*` variables are overridden to point at the Dracula tokens. Brand chain = pink ‚Üí purple ‚Üí pink-soft. Tip = cyan, warning = yellow, danger = red.
- `--vp-font-family-base` set to IBM Plex Sans, `--vp-font-family-mono` to IBM Plex Mono.
- Buttons forced to 2px corners.
- Hero: gradient swapped to `pink ‚Üí purple ‚Üí cyan` at `160deg`. Scanline overlay via `::after` at 4% opacity. Blink cursor `‚ñà` after the hero name via `.VPHero .name::after` with a 1.06s `steps(2,end)` keyframe.
- Feature cards: 4px corners, 1px divider outline, lift on hover via `translateY(-2px)`, outline brightens to purple on hover.
- Custom blocks: 4px corners, per-type accent titles (cyan / yellow / red).
- Code blocks: 4px corners, language label restyled to Plex Mono uppercase pink at top-right.
- Inline code: pink-tinted background dark / purple-tinted light.
- Header: 1px purple-25% bottom border.
- Tables: alternating row backgrounds, no row dividers, header row in `--bg-soft`.
- Headings: `-0.015em` letter-spacing on h1/h2, h1 weight 700.
- `::selection`: pink in dark, orange in light.
- Scrollbar: cyan thumb on `--bg-alt` track, both webkit and firefox declared.
- `:focus-visible`: 2px pink outline, 2px offset, no transition.
- Sidebar active item: pink text.
- Doc links: pink default, purple hover, animated underline colour transition only.

## docs/.vitepress/theme/index.ts
- Mermaid renderer theme arg: `dark` for dark mode, `neutral` (was `forest`) for light. Per the hardened design ‚Äî `forest` was the wrong palette family for the new identity.

## docs/.vitepress/config.ts
- `head` array adds:
  - `<link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.googleapis.com">`
  - `<link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.gstatic.com" crossorigin>`
  - `<link rel="stylesheet" href="...IBM Plex Sans + IBM Plex Mono">`
- Preconnect first, stylesheet second ‚Äî matches Google Fonts' own recommendation, gives the browser a head start on the TLS handshake before the actual stylesheet request.

## Verification
- `bun run docs:build`: completes cleanly, 15.78s build time.
- `bun test`: 3/3 pass.
- Site renders with Dracula identity in dark mode by default; light mode uses the desaturated derivation.

## Deliberate omissions vs the spec
None. Every quality gate from the hardened design is implemented.
local.quality_result:
# Quality Check Result

| # | Check | Status | Evidence |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Matches aesthetic direction precisely | ‚úÖ | Dracula tokens (`#ff79c6`, `#bd93f9`, `#8be9fd`, `#282a36`) present in built CSS. Border-radius rationing (2/4/999) implemented. Pink-as-brand wired through `--vp-c-brand-*`. |
| 2 | All specified animations present | ‚úÖ | `abtree-blink` keyframe present in built CSS. Hover lifts via `translateY(-2px)` on `.VPFeature`. Underline-colour transition on doc links. |
| 3 | Typography distinctive (not generic) | ‚úÖ | IBM Plex Sans + Plex Mono loaded via Google Fonts CDN with preconnect. Confirmed in `dist/index.html` head. `--vp-font-family-base` and `--vp-font-family-mono` overridden. No Inter/Roboto/Arial fallback before Plex. |
| 4 | Color palette committed fully | ‚úÖ | Both `:root` (light) and `.dark` blocks define every Dracula token. VitePress's brand/tip/warning/danger/default variables all forwarded to abtree tokens. |
| 5 | Spatial composition interesting | ‚úÖ | Feature cards: 4px corners, 1px outline, no shadow, hover lift. Tables: alternating row backgrounds, no row dividers. Header hairline in 25% purple. |
| 6 | Background/texture effects creating atmosphere | ‚úÖ | Hero scanline overlay via `::after` at 3px stripe, 4% opacity, `mix-blend-mode: overlay`. Light-mode variant with substrate-coloured stripes. |
| 7 | Clear memorable element | ‚úÖ | Animated `‚ñà` Plex Mono blink cursor following the hero name. 1.06s cadence matches xterm. Confirmed `abtree-blink` keyframe in built CSS. |
| 8 | Would pass as human-designed work from a top studio | ‚úÖ | Pink-as-primary instead of safe indigo. Plex commitment over system stack. Selection colour change between modes. Custom scrollbar in cyan. Code-block language label in Plex Mono small-caps pink. None of these read as default-VitePress. |

## Build verification
- `bun run docs:build`: clean, 15.78s.
- `bun test`: 3/3 pass.
- `dist/index.html`: contains preconnect + Plex stylesheet links.
- `dist/assets/style.*.css`: contains Dracula hex tokens, blink keyframe, IBM Plex font-family.

## Final assessment
All eight checks pass. No fixes required. The implementation precisely matches the hardened design.
